name: Ashnan
description: Ashnan is the Sumerian goddess of grain and sustenance. She is considered one of the most important deities in the Sumerian pantheon, responsible for providing the people with food and ensuring the fertility of the land. Ashnan is often depicted as a benevolent and nurturing figure, deeply connected to the earth and the cycles of agriculture. Her origins are intertwined with the creation myths, where she emerged as a vital component of the world's sustenance. She is believed to have the power to influence harvests, protect crops from blight, and guarantee a plentiful food supply for humanity and the gods. Ashnan's role is crucial in maintaining the balance of the cosmos and ensuring the survival of civilization. She is frequently invoked in prayers and rituals aimed at securing bountiful harvests and warding off famine. She is also involved in a creation myth where she and her brother Lahar, the god of cattle, were created to provide food and clothing for the Anunnaki gods. However, their excessive drinking and quarreling led to their demotion and assignment to serve humankind. Ashnan represents the vital connection between humanity and the land, and her worship highlights the importance of agriculture in Sumerian society. She is a symbol of nourishment, fertility, and the life-sustaining power of grain.
subtitle: Goddess of Grain and Sustenance

keyMyths.title: The Debate Between Sheep and Grain
keyMyths.description: Ashnan and Lahar are created by the gods to provide food and clothing. They engage in a drunken quarrel over who is superior, leading to their assignment to serve humans.
keyMyths.source: The Debate Between Sheep and Grain

keyMyths.title: Ensuring a Bountiful Harvest
keyMyths.description: Farmers would offer prayers and sacrifices to Ashnan to ensure a good harvest. These rituals were crucial for the survival and prosperity of the Sumerian city-states.

keyMyths.title: Ashnan's Role in Creation
keyMyths.description: Ashnan's creation is described as a response to the gods' need for sustenance. She is seen as a vital element in the initial world order, providing the foundation for civilization.

sources.author: Samuel Noah Kramer
